question: Will the USA use a strike against a US vessel as an excuse to resume mainland airstrikes?
evidence: On May 6 and 8, 2026, U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornets disabled Iranian-flagged tankers in the Gulf of Oman.
On May 7, 2026, U.S. forces intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz, targeting Iranian military facilities in response.
No U.S. vessels have been attacked or damaged in these incidents.
The U.S. has not resumed mainland airstrikes in response to these actions.
searchQueries: USA airstrikes resumption after US vessel attack latest news May 2026
raw: {"yes_probability": 0.2, "confidence": "medium", "short_reason": "Recent U.S. military actions in the Gulf of Oman have been targeted at Iranian assets, not U.S. vessels.", "key_evidence": ["On May 6 and 8, 2026, U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornets disabled Iranian-flagged tankers in the Gulf of Oman.", "On May 7, 2026, U.S. forces intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz, targeting Iranian military facilities in response.", "No U.S. vessels have been attacked or damaged in these incidents.", "The U.S. has not resumed mainland airstrikes in response to these actions."], "source_urls": ["https://www.armyrecognition.com/news/navy-news/2026/us-navy-launches-first_f_a-18_strafing_attacks_against_iranian_tankers_in_gulf_of_oman", "https://www.militarytimes.com/news/your-navy/2026/05/08/us-forces-disable-iranian-flagged_tankers-trying-to-cross_blockade/", "https://www.defensenews.com/news/your-navy/2026/05/07/us-and-iran-exchange-fire-as_trump_says_war_will_be_over_quickly/"]}
